pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 9 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 36.01° → 9:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: Subject is a single townhouse-style unit within a row of identical semi-attached homes, marked by the orange bounding box (reticle centered on the unit). The roof is a standard 4-sided hip roof. Bounding box measured at ~192 × 250 px, but the actual roof occupies ~175 × 195 px (lower ~55 px of the box is driveway/yard). At 0.1879 ft/px, the roof plan-view rectangle is ~32.9 ft × 36.6 ft ≈ 1,205 sq ft. No valleys or rake edges visible — pure hip roof. Ridge estimated visually at ~80 px (~15 ft) running N–S through the center; 4 hip lines at ~60 px each (~45 ft total); eave perimeter = 2 × (32.9 + 36.6) ≈ 139 ft. Confidence is moderate-low due to partial tree obstruction on the south end and the tight urban townhouse setting making edge delineation somewhat difficult.

condition.rationale: The subject roof at 3561 E 102nd Ct, Thornton, CO 80229 is a multi-pitch hip-style roof with what appears to be dark gray/charcoal architectural shingles. Examining the full surface within the orange bounding box, the shingle color and texture are uniform across all visible slopes — no discoloration patches, staining streaks, or color mismatches are present. Ridge lines and valley intersections appear geometrically straight with no visible sagging or deflection. There are no tarps, foreign debris, or large-scale missing material exposing decking. No green or dark biological growth patches are detectable on any slope. The roof's appearance is consistent with the immediately neighboring units, which share the same shingle type and color, further confirming the surface has no standout anomalies. No findings meet the confidence threshold for reporting at this resolution.

fence_reason: vision roof area 1506 differs from solar 2081 by 27.6% (>12%)
